questions.sentence: She has worked here _____ ten years.
questions.explanation: On utilise 'for' + durée (ten years). 'Since' est suivi d'un point de départ (ex: since 2014).

questions.sentence: I _____ to London last summer.
questions.explanation: 'Last summer' indique un moment passé précis → on utilise le simple past : 'went'.

questions.sentence: They _____ lunch when I arrived.
questions.explanation: Action en cours dans le passé interrompue par une autre action → past continuous : 'were having'.

questions.sentence: He _____ here since 2019.
questions.explanation: 'Since 2019' indique une action commencée dans le passé et qui continue → present perfect : 'has worked'.

questions.sentence: If it _____ tomorrow, we will cancel the trip.
questions.explanation: Dans une phrase conditionnelle de type 1 (if + présent, will + base verbale), on utilise le présent simple après 'if'.

questions.sentence: She is _____ than her sister.
questions.explanation: Pour les adjectifs courts (1 syllabe), on ajoute -er pour le comparatif : 'taller'.

questions.sentence: I have never _____ sushi before.
questions.explanation: Avec le present perfect (have + past participle), on utilise le participe passé : 'eaten'.

questions.sentence: She _____ to the gym every Monday.
questions.explanation: 'Every Monday' indique une habitude → présent simple. Avec 'she' (3e personne) : 'goes'.

questions.sentence: We _____ the project by next Friday.
questions.explanation: 'By next Friday' indique un moment futur → futur simple : 'will finish'.

questions.sentence: There _____ a lot of people at the meeting yesterday.
questions.explanation: 'A lot of people' est pluriel et 'yesterday' indique le passé → 'were'.

questions.sentence: I don't have _____ money left.
questions.explanation: Dans une phrase négative (don't have), on utilise 'any'. 'Some' est pour les phrases affirmatives.

questions.sentence: She told me _____ she was tired.
questions.explanation: 'Tell someone that...' est la structure correcte pour rapporter des paroles. 'That' introduit la proposition subordonnée.

questions.sentence: We _____ TV when the power went out.
questions.explanation: Action en cours dans le passé interrompue → past continuous : 'were watching'.

questions.sentence: My brother _____ three languages fluently.
questions.explanation: Capacité permanente → présent simple. 3e personne du singulier : 'speaks'.

questions.sentence: I _____ my keys. I can't find them anywhere.
questions.explanation: Résultat présent d'une action passée (je ne les trouve plus maintenant) → present perfect : 'have lost'.

questions.sentence: When I was a child, I _____ play outside every day.
questions.explanation: 'Used to + base verbale' décrit une habitude passée qui n'existe plus. 'Was used to' = avait l'habitude (différent).

questions.sentence: The train _____ at 9:15 every morning.
questions.explanation: Horaire fixe → présent simple. 3e personne : 'leaves'.

questions.sentence: Look! It _____!
questions.explanation: 'Look!' indique une situation en cours en ce moment → present continuous : 'is raining'.

questions.sentence: She _____ to Paris twice this year.
questions.explanation: 'This year' = période non terminée → present perfect : 'has been'.

questions.sentence: I _____ dinner before she arrived.
questions.explanation: Action terminée avant une autre action passée → past perfect : 'had cooked'.

questions.sentence: This is the _____ film I have ever seen.
questions.explanation: 'The best + ever' → superlatif. 'Good' → 'better' (comparatif) → 'best' (superlatif). 'Most good' n'existe pas.

questions.sentence: _____ you like some coffee?
questions.explanation: 'Would you like...?' est la forme polie pour proposer quelque chose. 'Do you like...?' demande si on aime en général.

questions.sentence: Can you give me some _____ about the new project?
questions.explanation: 'Information' est un nom indénombrable en anglais. Il ne prend jamais de 's' et ne peut pas être précédé de 'a/an'.

questions.sentence: I need to _____ an appointment with the doctor.
questions.explanation: On dit 'make an appointment' (prendre rendez-vous). 'Do' et 'make' sont souvent confondus par les francophones.

questions.sentence: The _____ from Paris to London takes about two hours by train.
questions.explanation: 'Journey' désigne un trajet d'un point A à un point B. 'Travel' est plus général (le fait de voyager). 'Voyage' est rare en anglais moderne.

questions.sentence: I'm looking _____ a new job.
questions.explanation: 'Look for' = chercher. 'Look at' = regarder. 'Look after' = s'occuper de.

questions.sentence: She speaks English very _____.
questions.explanation: Après un verbe, on utilise un adverbe (well), pas un adjectif (good). 'She speaks well' mais 'Her English is good'.

questions.sentence: I _____ a mistake in my email.
questions.explanation: On dit 'make a mistake' (faire une erreur), pas 'do a mistake'. C'est une collocation fixe en anglais.

questions.sentence: Could you _____ me the salt, please?
questions.explanation: 'Pass me the salt' est l'expression courante à table. 'Give' est aussi acceptable mais 'pass' est plus naturel.

questions.sentence: He is very _____ in music.
questions.explanation: 'Interested in' = s'intéresser à (la personne ressent l'intérêt). 'Interesting' = intéressant (la chose provoque l'intérêt).

questions.sentence: I'm sorry, I didn't _____ what you said. Could you repeat?
questions.explanation: 'Hear' = entendre (perception). 'Listen' = écouter (action volontaire). 'Attend' est un faux ami (= assister à).

questions.sentence: We need to _____ the homework before Friday.
questions.explanation: 'Do homework' (pas 'make'). 'Do' s'utilise avec les tâches : do the dishes, do exercise, do homework.

questions.sentence: She is very _____ of spiders.
questions.explanation: 'Scared of' = avoir peur de (la personne ressent la peur). 'Scary' = effrayant (la chose fait peur).

questions.sentence: I need some _____ about the train schedule.
questions.explanation: 'Advice' est indénombrable (comme 'information'). 'Advise' est le verbe. On dit 'some advice' ou 'a piece of advice'.

questions.sentence: The weather is really _____ today. Let's stay inside.
questions.explanation: Après 'is' (verbe d'état), on utilise un adjectif : 'bad'. 'Badly' est un adverbe (he plays badly).

questions.sentence: Can I _____ your phone? Mine is dead.
questions.explanation: 'Borrow' = emprunter (prendre). 'Lend' = prêter (donner temporairement). Confusion fréquente pour les francophones.

questions.sentence: We had a great _____ on holiday in Spain.
questions.explanation: 'Have a great time' = passer un bon moment. 'Moment' ne s'utilise pas de cette façon en anglais.

questions.sentence: The children are _____ in the garden.
questions.explanation: 'Play' = jouer. Les enfants jouent dans le jardin. En français on dit parfois 'faire' mais en anglais c'est 'play'.

questions.sentence: I usually _____ to work by bus.
questions.explanation: 'Go to work' = aller au travail. 'Come' implique un mouvement vers l'interlocuteur.

questions.sentence: He wants to _____ a photo of the sunset.
questions.explanation: 'Take a photo' = prendre une photo. Collocation fixe en anglais.

questions.sentence: _____ the rain, we decided to go out.
questions.explanation: 'Despite' + nom/gérondif (the rain). 'Although' serait suivi d'une proposition (although it was raining).

questions.sentence: She is good _____ maths.
questions.explanation: 'Good at' + matière/activité est la préposition correcte. C'est une collocation fixe.

questions.sentence: He depends _____ his parents for money.
questions.explanation: 'Depend on' = dépendre de. La préposition 'on' est obligatoire avec ce verbe.

questions.sentence: The meeting was cancelled. _____, we had time to prepare.
questions.explanation: 'However' s'utilise en début de phrase, suivi d'une virgule, pour introduire un contraste. 'But' ne commence généralement pas une phrase formelle.

questions.sentence: I'll wait _____ you finish.
questions.explanation: 'Until' + proposition (you finish) indique 'jusqu'à ce que'. 'By' indique une date limite. 'During' + nom.

questions.sentence: She insisted _____ paying the bill.
questions.explanation: 'Insist on + -ing' est la structure correcte. C'est une préposition fixée au verbe 'insist'.

questions.sentence: I'm not used _____ waking up early.
questions.explanation: 'Be used to + -ing' = avoir l'habitude de. Attention : 'used to + base verbale' = avoir l'habitude de (dans le passé).

questions.sentence: He _____ me that he would be late.
questions.explanation: 'Tell someone something' (avec complément d'objet indirect). 'Say something (to someone)' (sans COI direct).

questions.sentence: _____ I was tired, I finished the report.
questions.explanation: 'Although' + sujet + verbe (proposition complète). 'Despite' serait suivi d'un nom : 'Despite my tiredness'.

questions.sentence: The report must be ready _____ Friday.
questions.explanation: 'By + date' = au plus tard à cette date (deadline). 'Until' = pendant toute la durée jusqu'à.

questions.sentence: She apologized _____ being late.
questions.explanation: 'Apologize for + -ing' = s'excuser de. La préposition 'for' est obligatoire avec 'apologize'.

questions.sentence: I'm really looking forward _____ the concert.
questions.explanation: 'Look forward to' + nom ou gérondif. Attention : ici 'to' est une préposition, pas la marque de l'infinitif.

questions.sentence: He succeeded _____ passing the exam.
questions.explanation: 'Succeed in + -ing' = réussir à. Préposition fixe en anglais.

questions.sentence: She suffers _____ headaches.
questions.explanation: 'Suffer from' = souffrir de. Préposition obligatoire avec ce verbe.

questions.sentence: I'm thinking _____ changing jobs.
questions.explanation: 'Think about + -ing' = envisager de. 'Think of' est aussi possible mais 'about' est plus courant pour les plans.

questions.sentence: She said she was tired. _____, she continued working.
questions.explanation: 'Nevertheless' = néanmoins. Comme 'However', il s'utilise en début de phrase pour marquer le contraste. 'Despite of' n'existe pas.

questions.sentence: I haven't seen her _____ Monday.
questions.explanation: 'Since' + point de départ (Monday). 'For' + durée. 'Since Monday' = depuis lundi.

questions.sentence: He's interested _____ learning Spanish.
questions.explanation: 'Interested in + -ing' = intéressé par. Préposition fixe.

questions.sentence: We arrived _____ the airport two hours early.
questions.explanation: 'Arrive at' + lieu précis (airport, station). 'Arrive in' + ville/pays. On ne dit pas 'arrive to'.

questions.sentence: _____ having a map, we got lost.
questions.explanation: 'Despite + -ing / nom' = malgré. 'Although' serait suivi de 'we had a map'.

questions.sentence: We need to send the _____ to the client before the end of the month.
questions.explanation: 'Invoice' = facture (contexte business/B2B). 'Bill' = addition/facture (restaurant, services). 'Receipt' = reçu.

questions.sentence: The _____ for this project is next Monday.
questions.explanation: 'Deadline' = date limite. Mot très courant en anglais business.

questions.sentence: Our main _____ delivers raw materials from China.
questions.explanation: 'Supplier' = fournisseur. 'Customer' = client. Ne pas confondre les deux.

questions.sentence: We need to _____ a meeting to discuss the budget.
questions.explanation: 'Set up a meeting' = organiser une réunion. C'est un phrasal verb très courant en contexte professionnel.

questions.sentence: Please find _____ the document you requested.
questions.explanation: 'Please find attached' = veuillez trouver ci-joint. Expression standard dans les emails professionnels.

questions.sentence: I'd like to _____ a complaint about the service.
questions.explanation: 'Make a complaint' = porter une réclamation. Collocation fixe avec 'make'.

questions.sentence: The company's _____ has increased by 15% this year.
questions.explanation: 'Turnover' = chiffre d'affaires. 'Income' = revenu (personnel). 'Salary' = salaire (mensuel). 'Wage' = salaire (horaire).

questions.sentence: Could you _____ me back when you have a moment?
questions.explanation: 'Call someone back' = rappeler quelqu'un. Phrasal verb très courant au téléphone.

questions.sentence: We need to _____ with the new regulations.
questions.explanation: 'Comply with' = se conformer à. 'Apply' = postuler/appliquer. 'Supply' = fournir. 'Imply' = impliquer/sous-entendre.

questions.sentence: The manager asked us to _____ in the feedback form.
questions.explanation: 'Fill in a form' (UK) ou 'fill out a form' (US) = remplir un formulaire. 'Fill up' = remplir un réservoir.

questions.sentence: I need to _____ a decision by tomorrow.
questions.explanation: 'Make a decision' = prendre une décision. Collocation fixe avec 'make'.

questions.sentence: She works in the sales _____.
questions.explanation: 'Department' = service/département dans une entreprise. 'Service' est un faux ami fréquent.

questions.sentence: We signed the _____ with the new client yesterday.
questions.explanation: 'Contract' = contrat. 'Compromise' = compromis (faux ami). 'Deal' = accord (plus informel).

questions.sentence: The _____ of the meeting is to discuss Q3 results.
questions.explanation: 'Purpose' = objectif, but. 'The purpose of the meeting' = l'objet de la réunion. Expression business standard.

questions.sentence: He gave a very interesting _____ at the conference.
questions.explanation: 'Presentation' = présentation (exposé). 'Reunion' est un faux ami (= retrouvailles). 'Conference' = conférence (l'événement entier).

questions.sentence: I'll send you the report as an email _____.
questions.explanation: 'Attachment' = pièce jointe (email). 'Joint' et 'annex' ne s'utilisent pas dans ce contexte.

questions.sentence: We need to _____ a profit this quarter.
questions.explanation: 'Make a profit' = réaliser un bénéfice. Collocation fixe en business.

questions.sentence: The company has 500 _____ worldwide.
questions.explanation: 'Employees' = employés. 'Employers' = employeurs (ceux qui embauchent). 'Staff' est indénombrable.

questions.sentence: Could you _____ me the latest figures?
questions.explanation: 'Tell me' = dis-moi / donne-moi (des informations). 'Say' n'est pas suivi directement d'un COI.

questions.sentence: I'm writing to _____ about your recent order.
questions.explanation: 'Inquire about' = se renseigner sur. Registre formel, très utilisé dans les emails business. 'Demand' = exiger.

questions.sentence: You _____ have told me earlier!
questions.explanation: 'Should have + past participle' exprime un reproche sur le passé (tu aurais dû me le dire).

questions.sentence: She _____ have left already. Her car is not here.
questions.explanation: 'Must have + past participle' exprime une déduction forte sur le passé (elle a dû partir).

questions.sentence: The report _____ by the end of the day.
questions.explanation: Voix passive avec modal : 'must be + past participle'. Le rapport est l'objet de l'action (il est terminé par quelqu'un).

questions.sentence: Could you tell me where the station _____?
questions.explanation: Question indirecte : l'ordre est sujet + verbe (where the station is), pas l'ordre inversé d'une question directe.

questions.sentence: Do you know what time the meeting _____?
questions.explanation: Question indirecte → ordre affirmatif : 'what time the meeting starts' (pas d'inversion, pas d'auxiliaire 'does').

questions.sentence: The new office _____ next month.
questions.explanation: Voix passive au futur : 'will be + past participle'. Le bureau est ouvert par quelqu'un (l'entreprise).

questions.sentence: He _____ be at work. It's Sunday.
questions.explanation: 'Can't' exprime l'impossibilité logique (il ne peut pas être au travail, c'est dimanche). 'Mustn't' = interdiction.

questions.sentence: I wonder if she _____ come to the party.
questions.explanation: Question indirecte au futur : 'I wonder if she will come'. Pas d'inversion dans la question indirecte.

questions.sentence: English _____ in many countries around the world.
questions.explanation: Voix passive au présent : 'is + past participle'. L'anglais est parlé (par les gens) → passif.

questions.sentence: You _____ smoke in the office. It's forbidden.
questions.explanation: 'Mustn't' = interdiction. 'Don't have to' = pas obligatoire (mais permis). 'Shouldn't' = déconseillé.

questions.sentence: The window _____ by the children yesterday.
questions.explanation: Passif au passé : 'was + past participle'. La fenêtre a été cassée par les enfants.

questions.sentence: You _____ wear a suit. It's a casual event.
questions.explanation: 'Don't have to' = pas obligatoire (tu n'es pas obligé). 'Mustn't' = c'est interdit. Ici c'est juste non obligatoire.

questions.sentence: Could you tell me how much this _____?
questions.explanation: Question indirecte → pas d'inversion, pas d'auxiliaire : 'how much this costs'.

questions.sentence: She _____ have been very tired. She slept for twelve hours.
questions.explanation: 'Must have been' = elle devait être (déduction logique forte basée sur une preuve : 12 heures de sommeil).

questions.sentence: The house _____ in 1920.
questions.explanation: Passif au passé avec date précise (1920) → 'was built'.

questions.sentence: He asked me where I _____.
questions.explanation: Discours indirect : le verbe recule d'un temps. 'Where do you live?' → 'where I lived' (concordance des temps).

questions.sentence: You _____ to see a doctor. That cough sounds bad.
questions.explanation: 'Ought to' = devrait (conseil). Synonyme de 'should' mais plus formel. 'Ought' est toujours suivi de 'to'.

questions.sentence: I'd like to know whether the shop _____ on Sundays.
questions.explanation: Question indirecte. 'Is open' décrit un état. 'Whether the shop is open' = si le magasin est ouvert.

questions.sentence: This cake _____ by my grandmother.
questions.explanation: Voix passive : 'was made by' = a été fait par. Le complément d'agent est introduit par 'by'.

questions.sentence: She _____ be French. She has a Parisian accent.
questions.explanation: 'Might' exprime la possibilité : elle pourrait être française (on n'est pas sûr, mais c'est possible).

questions.sentence: We need to _____ the issue before it gets worse.
questions.explanation: 'Address an issue' = traiter/aborder un problème. Faux ami : 'assist' = aider, 'attend' = assister à.

questions.sentence: The project is _____ schedule. We'll finish early.
questions.explanation: 'Ahead of schedule' = en avance sur le planning. Expression business très courante.

questions.sentence: Please _____ me if you have any questions.
questions.explanation: La phrase complète est 'Please let me know' mais ici le 'me' est déjà dans la phrase. 'Let know' = informer/prévenir.

questions.sentence: I'd like to _____ a suggestion regarding the new policy.
questions.explanation: 'Make a suggestion' = faire une suggestion. Collocation fixe avec 'make'.

questions.sentence: We need to _____ costs if we want to remain profitable.
questions.explanation: 'Cut costs' est la collocation la plus naturelle en business. 'Reduce costs' est aussi acceptable.

questions.sentence: She was _____ for the position of marketing director.
questions.explanation: 'Hired' = embauchée. 'Fired' = licenciée. 'Retired' = retraitée. 'Resigned' = a démissionné.

questions.sentence: The company decided to _____ its operations to Asia.
questions.explanation: 'Expand operations' = développer/étendre ses activités. Collocation courante en business.

questions.sentence: We should _____ advantage of this opportunity.
questions.explanation: 'Take advantage of' = profiter de, tirer parti de. Expression figée.

questions.sentence: The sales team managed to _____ their targets this quarter.
questions.explanation: 'Meet targets' = atteindre les objectifs. 'Reach a goal' et 'achieve a goal' sont aussi corrects mais 'meet targets' est la collocation standard.

questions.sentence: I'm _____ forward to hearing from you.
questions.explanation: 'Look forward to + -ing' = avoir hâte de / attendre avec impatience. Expression très courante dans les emails.

questions.sentence: We need to _____ an agreement before the end of the week.
questions.explanation: 'Reach an agreement' = parvenir à un accord. Collocation standard en négociation.

questions.sentence: The project was completed on time and within _____.
questions.explanation: 'Within budget' = dans le budget. Expression standard en gestion de projet.

questions.sentence: I need to _____ a report on last month's sales.
questions.explanation: 'Write a report' = rédiger un rapport. Collocation la plus naturelle.

questions.sentence: She is in _____ of the marketing department.
questions.explanation: 'In charge of' = responsable de. Expression très courante en contexte professionnel.

questions.sentence: We had a productive _____ with the new client.
questions.explanation: 'Meeting' = réunion professionnelle. 'Reunion' = retrouvailles (faux ami). 'Appointment' = rendez-vous (médecin, etc.).

questions.sentence: The company provides excellent _____ for its employees.
questions.explanation: 'Benefits' = avantages sociaux (assurance, congés, etc.). 'Advantages' est plus général. 'Profits' = bénéfices financiers.

questions.sentence: Could you give me an _____ of how much this will cost?
questions.explanation: 'Estimate' = estimation, devis. 'Give an estimate' est la collocation standard en business.

questions.sentence: The _____ shows that most customers prefer online shopping.
questions.explanation: 'Survey' = enquête, sondage. 'Research' = recherche (plus académique). 'Search' = recherche (action de chercher).

questions.sentence: Please _____ free to contact me if you need anything.
questions.explanation: 'Feel free to' = n'hésitez pas à. Expression de politesse très courante dans les emails professionnels.

questions.sentence: I wish I _____ more time to prepare for the presentation.
questions.explanation: 'I wish + past simple' exprime un regret sur le présent. 'I wish I had' = j'aimerais avoir (mais je n'ai pas).

questions.sentence: By the time we arrive, they _____ dinner.
questions.explanation: 'By the time + present, will have + past participle' = future perfect. L'action sera terminée avant notre arrivée.

questions.sentence: She avoids _____ in public.
questions.explanation: 'Avoid + -ing'. Certains verbes sont toujours suivis du gérondif : avoid, enjoy, mind, suggest, finish, etc.

questions.sentence: He promised _____ me with the project.
questions.explanation: 'Promise + to + infinitif'. Certains verbes sont suivis de l'infinitif : promise, decide, want, hope, agree, etc.

questions.sentence: If I _____ you, I would accept the offer.
questions.explanation: Conditionnel 2 (irréel du présent) : 'If I were you' (subjonctif). 'Were' est utilisé pour toutes les personnes dans cette structure.

questions.sentence: Not only _____ late, but he also forgot the documents.
questions.explanation: 'Not only' en début de phrase entraîne une inversion sujet-verbe : 'Not only was he late'.

questions.sentence: She suggested _____ the meeting to next week.
questions.explanation: 'Suggest + -ing' (pas d'infinitif). On peut aussi dire 'suggest that we postpone'.

questions.sentence: I'd rather _____ at home tonight.
questions.explanation: 'Would rather + base verbale' (infinitif sans 'to'). 'I'd rather stay' = je préférerais rester.

questions.sentence: The more you practise, the _____ you'll get.
questions.explanation: 'The more... the better' = plus on..., mieux c'est. Structure comparative parallèle.

questions.sentence: She denied _____ the email.
questions.explanation: 'Deny + -ing'. Comme 'avoid', 'deny' est toujours suivi du gérondif.

questions.sentence: I wish I _____ studied harder at university.
questions.explanation: 'I wish I had + past participle' exprime un regret sur le passé. 'I wish I had studied' = j'aurais aimé avoir étudié.

questions.sentence: She doesn't mind _____ overtime.
questions.explanation: 'Mind + -ing'. 'Don't mind working' = ça ne me dérange pas de travailler.

questions.sentence: He decided _____ the job offer.
questions.explanation: 'Decide + to + infinitif'. 'Decide' fait partie des verbes suivis de l'infinitif.

questions.sentence: If I had left earlier, I _____ the train.
questions.explanation: Conditionnel 3 (irréel du passé) : 'If I had + PP, I would have + PP'. Exprime un regret.

questions.sentence: I can't help _____ when I see that video.
questions.explanation: 'Can't help + -ing' = ne pas pouvoir s'empêcher de. Expression figée suivie du gérondif.

questions.sentence: She keeps _____ the same mistakes.
questions.explanation: 'Keep + -ing' = continuer à. 'She keeps making' = elle continue de faire.

questions.sentence: I expect him _____ on time.
questions.explanation: 'Expect someone to + infinitif'. Structure : verbe + COD + to + infinitif.

questions.sentence: It's no use _____ about it now.
questions.explanation: 'It's no use + -ing' = ça ne sert à rien de. Expression figée suivie du gérondif.

questions.sentence: She went on _____ even after everyone had left.
questions.explanation: 'Go on + -ing' = continuer à faire la même chose. 'Go on + to' = passer à l'étape suivante.

questions.sentence: I regret _____ you that the position has been filled.
questions.explanation: 'Regret + to + infinitif' = regretter de devoir (annonce formelle). 'Regret + -ing' = regretter d'avoir fait.

questions.sentence: The negotiations fell _____ at the last minute.
questions.explanation: 'Fall through' = échouer, tomber à l'eau (pour un accord, un projet). 'Fall down' = tomber physiquement.

questions.sentence: We need to come _____ with a solution quickly.
questions.explanation: 'Come up with' = trouver, proposer (une idée, une solution). Phrasal verb très courant.

questions.sentence: I'll look _____ the matter and get back to you.
questions.explanation: 'Look into' = examiner, enquêter sur. 'Look at' = regarder. 'Look for' = chercher.

questions.sentence: They had to put _____ the meeting because of the storm.
questions.explanation: 'Put off' = reporter, remettre à plus tard. 'Put on' = mettre (un vêtement). 'Put up with' = supporter.

questions.sentence: It took me a while to figure _____ how the new system works.
questions.explanation: 'Figure out' = comprendre, trouver la solution. Phrasal verb très courant.

questions.sentence: The CEO stepped _____ after the scandal.
questions.explanation: 'Step down' = démissionner (d'un poste de responsabilité). 'Step up' = prendre les choses en main.

questions.sentence: We ran _____ of paper, so I need to order more.
questions.explanation: 'Run out of' = être à court de, manquer de. 'We ran out of paper' = on n'a plus de papier.

questions.sentence: She turned _____ the job offer because the salary was too low.
questions.explanation: 'Turn down' = refuser (une offre, une proposition). 'Turn off' = éteindre.

questions.sentence: We need to carry _____ the plan despite the difficulties.
questions.explanation: 'Carry out' = exécuter, réaliser (un plan, une tâche). 'Carry on' = continuer.

questions.sentence: I need to catch _____ on my emails after the holiday.
questions.explanation: 'Catch up on' = rattraper son retard sur. 'Catch up with someone' = retrouver quelqu'un / rattraper quelqu'un.

questions.sentence: The meeting was called _____ due to the director's illness.
questions.explanation: 'Call off' = annuler. 'The meeting was called off' = la réunion a été annulée.

questions.sentence: He brought _____ an interesting point during the discussion.
questions.explanation: 'Bring up' = soulever (un sujet, un point). Phrasal verb courant en contexte professionnel.

questions.sentence: We need to sort _____ this problem immediately.
questions.explanation: 'Sort out' = résoudre, régler (un problème). Très courant en anglais britannique.

questions.sentence: She gets _____ well with all her colleagues.
questions.explanation: 'Get on well with' (UK) / 'Get along with' (US) = bien s'entendre avec.

questions.sentence: I can't put _____ with his behavior anymore.
questions.explanation: 'Put up with' = supporter, tolérer. 'I can't put up with it' = je ne le supporte plus.

questions.sentence: The new law comes _____ effect next month.
questions.explanation: 'Come into effect' = entrer en vigueur. Expression standard en contexte juridique/business.

questions.sentence: Let's go _____ the main points of the meeting.
questions.explanation: 'Go over' = passer en revue, revoir. 'Let's go over the main points' = passons en revue les points principaux.

questions.sentence: The factory had to lay _____ 200 workers.
questions.explanation: 'Lay off' = licencier (pour raisons économiques). Différent de 'fire' (licencier pour faute).

questions.sentence: She takes _____ her mother. They look very similar.
questions.explanation: 'Take after' = ressembler à (un parent). 'Take on' = embaucher/accepter. 'Take over' = reprendre (une entreprise).

questions.sentence: We need to cut _____ on unnecessary expenses.
questions.explanation: 'Cut down on' = réduire (la consommation de quelque chose). 'Cut off' = couper (l'électricité, la communication).

questions.sentence: Had I known about the problem, I _____ acted sooner.
questions.explanation: Conditionnel 3 avec inversion : 'Had I known... I would have acted'. Équivaut à 'If I had known'.

questions.sentence: It's high time we _____ a decision.
questions.explanation: 'It's high time + past simple' exprime qu'il est plus que temps de faire quelque chose. Le passé a ici valeur de subjonctif.

questions.sentence: Hardly _____ the presentation when the fire alarm went off.
questions.explanation: 'Hardly + inversion (had he started) + when...' = à peine avait-il commencé que... Structure littéraire avec inversion obligatoire.

questions.sentence: The project, _____ was started last year, is finally complete.
questions.explanation: Proposition relative non restrictive (entre virgules) → on utilise 'which', jamais 'that'.

questions.sentence: She acts as if she _____ the boss.
questions.explanation: 'As if + past subjunctive (were)' pour exprimer une situation irréelle. Même au singulier, on préfère 'were' dans un style formel.

questions.sentence: No sooner had we left _____ it started raining.
questions.explanation: 'No sooner... than' = à peine... que. C'est une paire fixe. Ne pas confondre avec 'hardly... when'.

questions.sentence: I would rather you _____ this to anyone.
questions.explanation: 'Would rather + someone + past simple' pour exprimer une préférence concernant l'action d'une autre personne.

questions.sentence: _____ the circumstances, I think we did well.
questions.explanation: 'Given the circumstances' = étant donné les circonstances. 'Given' fonctionne ici comme une préposition.

questions.sentence: The data _____ that sales have dropped significantly.
questions.explanation: 'Data' peut être singulier ou pluriel en anglais moderne. Avec 'suggests' (singulier), c'est l'usage le plus courant en business.

questions.sentence: Were it not _____ your help, we would have failed.
questions.explanation: 'Were it not for' = sans (s'il n'y avait pas eu). Inversion formelle équivalente à 'If it were not for'.

questions.sentence: Little _____ he know what was about to happen.
questions.explanation: 'Little did he know' = il ne savait pas du tout. Inversion avec 'little' en début de phrase (registre littéraire).

questions.sentence: _____ should you try to fix this alone.
questions.explanation: 'On no account' + inversion = en aucun cas. Expression formelle avec inversion obligatoire du sujet et de l'auxiliaire.

questions.sentence: It is essential that he _____ present at the meeting.
questions.explanation: Subjonctif mandatif après 'essential that' : base verbale sans conjugaison. 'It is essential that he be' (anglais formel).

questions.sentence: _____ had the meeting started than the CEO walked in.
questions.explanation: 'No sooner... than'. 'Hardly/Scarcely/Barely' sont suivis de 'when', pas de 'than'.

questions.sentence: She spoke _____ convincingly that everyone believed her.
questions.explanation: 'So + adverbe + that' = tellement... que. 'Such' précède un nom : 'such a convincing speech that...'.

questions.sentence: Not until the deadline passed _____ realize his mistake.
questions.explanation: 'Not until...' en début de phrase → inversion obligatoire : 'did he realize'. Structure emphatique formelle.

questions.sentence: He behaves as though nothing _____.
questions.explanation: 'As though + past perfect' pour une situation irréelle dans le passé. Plus formel que 'as if'.

questions.sentence: _____ interesting the book is, I can't finish it by tomorrow.
questions.explanation: 'However + adjectif' = aussi... que ce soit. 'However interesting' = aussi intéressant soit-il.

questions.sentence: The report needs _____.
questions.explanation: 'Need + -ing' a un sens passif : 'needs rewriting' = a besoin d'être réécrit. Équivalent de 'needs to be rewritten'.

questions.sentence: So _____ was the damage that the building had to be demolished.
questions.explanation: 'So + adjective + inversion' pour emphase. 'Extensive damage' = dégâts importants. Collocation naturelle.

questions.sentence: The two companies decided to _____ forces to enter the Asian market.
questions.explanation: 'Join forces' = unir ses forces, s'allier. Expression idiomatique. 'Merge' = fusionner (devenir une seule entité).

questions.sentence: The new policy has had a significant _____ on employee morale.
questions.explanation: 'Have an impact on' = avoir un impact sur. 'Affect' est un verbe, 'effect' est un nom. 'Impact' est le plus naturel avec 'have a... on'.

questions.sentence: They need to _____ the gap between theory and practice.
questions.explanation: 'Bridge the gap' = combler l'écart. Expression idiomatique très courante.

questions.sentence: His behavior at the meeting was completely _____.
questions.explanation: 'Unacceptable' est le seul préfixe correct. Les autres formes n'existent pas en anglais.

questions.sentence: We should _____ the pros and cons before making a decision.
questions.explanation: 'Weigh the pros and cons' = peser le pour et le contre. Expression idiomatique standard.

questions.sentence: The company is going through a major _____.
questions.explanation: 'Overhaul' = refonte, révision en profondeur. 'Turnover' = chiffre d'affaires. 'Overthrow' = renverser (un gouvernement).

questions.sentence: She has a _____ knowledge of the industry.
questions.explanation: 'Thorough' = approfondi, minutieux. 'Through' = à travers. 'Throughout' = partout dans. 'Though' = cependant.

questions.sentence: The report highlighted several _____ in the current process.
questions.explanation: 'Shortcomings' = lacunes, insuffisances. 'Defects' = défauts techniques. 'Defaults' = paramètres par défaut.

questions.sentence: It's essential to maintain a good _____ with your colleagues.
questions.explanation: 'Relationship' = relation (entre personnes). 'Relation' est plus formel. 'Rapport' existe mais est moins courant.

questions.sentence: The board voted to _____ the proposal unanimously.
questions.explanation: 'Approve a proposal' = approuver une proposition. 'Prove' = prouver. 'Improve' = améliorer.

questions.sentence: The team showed great _____ in completing the project early.
questions.explanation: 'Commitment' = engagement, implication. 'Committee' = comité. 'Commission' = commission. Faux amis fréquents.

questions.sentence: We need a more _____ approach to this problem.
questions.explanation: 'Comprehensive' = global, complet. 'Comprehensible' = compréhensible. 'Apprehensive' = anxieux. Faux amis.

questions.sentence: The _____ between the two proposals is quite significant.
questions.explanation: 'Discrepancy' = écart, divergence (souvent entre chiffres ou versions). Plus précis que 'difference' dans ce contexte formel.

questions.sentence: She managed to _____ a good work-life balance.
questions.explanation: 'Strike a balance' = trouver un équilibre. Expression idiomatique. 'Achieve a balance' est aussi acceptable.

questions.sentence: The new regulations will come into _____ next January.
questions.explanation: 'Come into force' = entrer en vigueur (lois, règlements). 'Come into effect' est aussi correct.

questions.sentence: He has a _____ for detail that makes him perfect for the job.
questions.explanation: 'An eye for detail' = un sens du détail. Expression idiomatique.

questions.sentence: The company is at the _____ of innovation in its field.
questions.explanation: 'At the forefront of' = à la pointe de, en première ligne de. 'Cutting edge' est aussi courant.

questions.sentence: We must take _____ action to prevent further damage.
questions.explanation: 'Swift action' = action rapide. Collocation formelle. 'Quick' et 'fast' sont plus informels.

questions.sentence: The results were _____ our expectations.
questions.explanation: 'Beyond expectations' = au-delà des attentes. Expression standard pour dire que les résultats ont dépassé les prévisions.

questions.sentence: There's been a sharp _____ in demand for electric vehicles.
questions.explanation: 'A rise in' = une hausse de. 'Rise' = nom (hausse). 'Raise' = verbe transitif (augmenter qqch). 'Arise' = surgir.
